summary: The unauthenticated comment deletion vulnerability was confirmed through code review but a Docker-based reproduction was not built because the primary SQL injection finding was prioritized for PoC development.
expected_security_impact: Sending a POST request to /objects/commentDelete.json.php with an arbitrary id parameter should delete the specified comment without any authentication or authorization check.
actual_observed_result: Not attempted. The vulnerability is confirmed through static code analysis: objects/commentDelete.json.php contains no User::isLogged(), User::isAdmin(), or CSRF check before calling Comment::delete().
verification_method: Code review confirms the endpoint performs no authentication or authorization check. The exploit requires only a single HTTP request: curl -X POST 'http://target/objects/commentDelete.json.php' -d 'id=1'
evidence_produced_by_run: Code review: objects/commentDelete.json.php:24-26 creates a Comment object and calls delete() without any authentication or ownership check.
No User::isLogged(), User::isAdmin(), or isUntrustedRequest() call exists in the entire file.
limitations_or_blockers: PoC reproduction not attempted; primary SQL injection finding was prioritized for PoC development.
